label: pH minimum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality (class form) that describes the minimum pH value supporting growth. Companion to datatype property METPO:2000705.

label: pH maximum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality (class form) that describes the maximum pH value supporting growth. Companion to datatype property METPO:2000706.

label: temperature minimum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality (class form) that describes the minimum temperature supporting growth. Companion to datatype property METPO:2000702.

label: temperature maximum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality (class form) that describes the maximum temperature supporting growth. Companion to datatype property METPO:2000703.

label: salinity minimum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality (class form) that describes the minimum salinity/NaCl concentration supporting growth. Companion to datatype property METPO:2000708.

label: salinity maximum tolerance
definition: An environmental quality (class form) that describes the maximum salinity/NaCl concentration supporting growth. Companion to datatype property METPO:2000709.

label: flagellar arrangement
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the arrangement pattern of flagella on a bacterial or archaeal cell. Parent class for METPO:1005031-1005037 (peritrichous, polar, amphitrichous, lophotrichous, monotrichous, lateral, subpolar).

label: polytrichous flagellation
definition: Multiple flagella per cell (typically a tuft); often combined with polar attachment.

label: selective media growth capability
definition: A phenotypic capability describing the ability to grow on selective or differential media.

label: growth on MacConkey agar
definition: Capability to grow on MacConkey agar; differential medium typically used to distinguish lactose-fermenting Gram-negative bacteria.
synonyms: MacConkey agar growth|grows on MacConkey agar

label: growth on blood agar
definition: Capability to grow on blood agar; enriched medium typically used for fastidious organisms and hemolysis assessment.
synonyms: blood agar growth|grows on blood agar

label: growth on EMB agar
definition: Capability to grow on Eosin Methylene Blue (EMB) agar; selective and differential medium for Gram-negative enteric bacteria.
synonyms: EMB agar growth|Eosin Methylene Blue agar growth

label: bile acid response
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the organism's growth response when challenged with bile acids or bile salts. Encompasses both tolerance/resistance (growth proceeds) and susceptibility (growth is inhibited); the specific outcome is asserted by child classes.

label: bile acid susceptible
definition: Phenotype where growth is inhibited by bile acids or bile salts.

label: colony morphology
definition: A phenotypic quality describing macroscopic colony characteristics (shape, margin, elevation, surface, color, size).

label: colony shape
definition: Subaspect of colony morphology describing the overall macroscopic colony outline as observed on solid medium.

label: circular colony
definition: Colony with a regular round outline.

label: irregular colony
definition: Colony with an irregular (non-round, non-rhizoid) outline.

label: filamentous colony
definition: Colony with thread-like or filamentous outline.

label: punctiform colony
definition: Very small (pinpoint) colony, typically <1 mm diameter.

label: rhizoid colony
definition: Colony with branching, root-like outline.

label: fried-egg-shaped colony
definition: Colony with raised opaque centre and translucent peripheral zone, resembling a fried egg.

label: pressure tolerance
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the ability to grow under elevated hydrostatic pressure.

label: barophile phenotype
definition: An organism that requires or tolerates elevated hydrostatic pressure for growth.

label: radiation tolerance
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the ability to withstand ionizing or UV radiation.

label: osmotic tolerance
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the ability to grow under high osmotic pressure (non-NaCl).

label: metal tolerance
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the ability to grow in the presence of elevated metal concentrations.

label: capsule presence
definition: A phenotypic quality describing the presence of an extracellular polysaccharide capsule.

label: biofilm formation capability
definition: A phenotypic capability describing the ability to form surface-attached biofilms.

label: catalase test
definition: A biochemical test that detects catalase enzyme activity by exposing cells to hydrogen peroxide and observing for visible bubbling. The test outcome (positive or negative) is captured by its child classes; this class itself does not assert that the organism has catalase activity.

label: catalase positive
definition: Phenotype where the catalase test yields a positive result (visible bubbling on H2O2), indicating that the organism has detectable catalase activity (GO:0004096).

label: catalase negative
definition: Phenotype where the catalase test yields a negative result (no bubbling on H2O2), indicating absence of detectable catalase activity.

label: oxidase test
definition: A biochemical test that detects cytochrome c oxidase activity using a redox indicator. The test outcome (positive or negative) is captured by its child classes; this class itself does not assert oxidase activity.

label: oxidase positive
definition: Phenotype where the oxidase test yields a positive result, indicating detectable cytochrome c oxidase activity (GO:0004129).

label: oxidase negative
definition: Phenotype where the oxidase test yields a negative result, indicating absence of detectable cytochrome c oxidase activity.

label: urease test
definition: A biochemical test that detects urease enzyme activity by observing urea hydrolysis (typically via a pH-indicator color change). The test outcome (positive or negative) is captured by its child classes; this class itself does not assert urease activity.

label: urease positive
definition: Phenotype where the urease test yields a positive result, indicating detectable urease activity (GO:0009039).

label: urease negative
definition: Phenotype where the urease test yields a negative result, indicating absence of detectable urease activity.

label: coagulase activity
definition: A phenotypic quality describing coagulase enzyme activity (clotting of blood plasma). Diagnostic marker, e.g. for Staphylococcus aureus.

label: coagulase positive
definition: Phenotype where the coagulase test yields a positive result (plasma clotting).

label: coagulase negative
definition: Phenotype where the coagulase test yields a negative result.

label: xerophilic phenotype
definition: A phenotypic quality describing a microbe that thrives in low water-activity environments (typically aw < 0.85). Sibling concept of osmotic and metal tolerance; distinct from halophily because the limiting factor is water activity rather than salt concentration.

label: epibiont phenotype
definition: A phenotypic quality describing a microbe that lives on the external surface of a host organism or substrate, as distinct from endosymbionts (which live inside the host). Captures host-association mode, not specific host taxonomy.
